turns.content: alright I need you to settle a debate thats been goin on for like 3 days between me and my roommate
Three days is a long time to argue about something. What's the debate?
best pizza in boston. he says santarpios in east boston and I say reginas in the north end and were not backin down
That's a heavyweight matchup right there. Both of those are legendary spots. What's your case for Regina's over Santarpio's?

bro reginas has been there since like 1926 ok the crust is perfect every single time its got that char on the bottom the sauce is just right not too sweet not too acidic and you cant beat sittin in the original north end spot its just a vibe

The original North End location does have an atmosphere that's hard to beat. There's something about the history of the place, the cramped dining room, the no-frills approach. And you're right that the char on the crust is a signature. What's your roommate's argument for Santarpio's?

he says santarpios has better toppings and the sausage pizza is the best in the city and that the lamb skewers or whatever make it a better overall spot. which like ok the lamb things are good but were talkin about PIZZA not lamb

He's moving the goalposts if he's bringing lamb skewers into a pizza debate. If the question is strictly best pizza, you have to judge it on the pizza. That said, Santarpio's sausage pizza is legitimately excellent. The sausage is made in-house and the crust has its own thing going on. It's a closer call than either of you probably wants to admit.

see thats what everyone says but im tellin you if you put em side by side reginas wins every time. and dont even get me started on the people who say like boston doesnt have good pizza compared to new york cause that drives me insane

The New York comparison is a different argument entirely. New York has great pizza, but Boston has its own style and its own legendary spots. Comparing the two is like comparing apples and oranges — different crust styles, different traditions. Anyone who says Boston doesn't have good pizza hasn't been to the right places.
THANK YOU bro honestly. like reginas alone would shut that argument down. ok so for the record you agree with me right reginas number one

I'm not going to pick a side and get you in more trouble with your roommate. But I will say that Regina's has nearly a century of history backing it up and the original location is genuinely special. If you forced me to pick one slice, the char crust at Regina's is hard to argue against.

no cap thats all I needed to hear. and the thing is I brought like 4 different people there last month and every single one of em said it was the best pizza theyve ever had so its not just me bein biased its a fact at this point

That's a pretty strong sample group backing you up. When multiple people with no stake in the argument all come to the same conclusion, it's hard to call it bias. Your roommate might just have to accept that he's in the minority on this one.
im screenshottin this and sendin it to danny lol thats a W for me. alright thanks for the backup kid
Ha, use it wisely. Tell Danny no hard feelings — Santarpio's is still a great pick, just not quite number one.
